ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC 'Sausages and similar products' is the closest defensible parent; no specific code for Malaysian golden crust pan-fried sausage.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226789007 'Sausage (food)' used as closest parent; no specific code for this preparation style.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Golden Crust Sausage at a Malaysian kopitiam or coffee bar consists of approximately two pan-fried sausages, weighing around 100 g in total.

nutrient_source.title: Pork sausage, cooked — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 07065)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No specific entry for Malaysian golden crust sausage in HPB Singapore Foodo, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy cooked pork sausage (NDB 07065 / FDC 174608), which represents a comparable processed, pan-fried pork sausage. Sodium and fat values are consistent with typical Southeast Asian processed sausage products. Values should be updated if a direct regional source becomes available.

gi_source.title: Sausage, GI value from Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC — International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008
gi_source.organization: Diabetes Care, American Diabetes Association (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 28 is sourced from the International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care), which reports GI = 28 for sausage (glucose reference). This is used as the closest analogous estimate for Golden Crust Sausage, a pan-fried processed sausage with very low carbohydrate content (1.5 g per 100 g). Glycemic load per 100 g serving = (28 × 1.5) / 100 = 0.42, rounded to 0.4.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (749 mg per 100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of Golden Crust Sausage due to its high sodium content of approximately 749 mg per 100 g, which can contribute significantly to daily sodium intake and elevate blood pressure; portion size should be strictly controlled and paired with low-sodium foods.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High saturated fat and processed meat classification
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although the glycemic index of Golden Crust Sausage is low (GI ≈ 28) due to minimal carbohydrate content, its high saturated fat content (9.4 g per 100 g) and processed meat classification are associated with increased insulin resistance and cardiovascular risk in individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, and consumption should be limited to occasional servings.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat, high sodium, and processed meat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Golden Crust Sausage presents a high cardiovascular concern due to its combination of elevated saturated fat (9.4 g per 100 g), high sodium (749 mg per 100 g), and cholesterol (70 mg per 100 g); individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should minimise intake in line with dietary guidelines recommending reduced processed meat consumption.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Golden Crust Sausage is a pan-fried or grilled processed sausage dish popular in Malaysian kopitiams (traditional coffee shops) and coffee bars in Singapore, such as EC Coffee Bar on Carpenter Street. The sausage is cooked until the outer casing develops a characteristic golden, crispy crust while the interior remains juicy and savoury. It is typically made from pork or chicken, seasoned with salt, pepper, and spices, and served as a breakfast or snack item alongside kaya toast, soft-boiled eggs, or white coffee. Nutritionally, Golden Crust Sausage is energy-dense at approximately 301 kcal per 100 g, with a high fat content (26.8 g, of which 9.4 g is saturated fat) and moderate protein (13.6 g). Carbohydrate content is very low (1.5 g per 100 g), resulting in a low glycemic index of approximately 28. Sodium content is high at 749 mg per 100 g, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. As a processed meat product, it is associated with increased cardiovascular and metabolic risk when consumed frequently. It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those following a halal diet unless a certified halal variant is specified.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Golden Crust Sausage?
What is the GI of sausage?
meal_context: Served with kaya toast and soft-boiled eggs

confidence_reason: No direct entry for Golden Crust Sausage was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy cooked pork sausage (FDC 174608), which is a comparable processed pan-fried sausage. GI is estimated from the International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values (Atkinson et al., 2008). Confidence is medium because the adaptation is defensible but not an exact match to this specific product.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for cooked pork sausage (FDC 174608); no direct regional database entry was found for this specific product. Values should be updated if IMR Malaysia MyFCD or HPB Singapore Foodo publishes a matching entry. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for this food. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for this food. 3. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 as confirmed by the USDA source for cooked pork sausage; this is a confirmed zero per the approved source. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0 as reported in the USDA source. 4. GI of 28 is an analogous estimate from Atkinson et al. (2008) International Tables of GI and GL Values, Diabetes Care; gi_category is low (GI ≤ 55). 5. Halal status is not confirmed; the food is served at EC Coffee Bar which may use pork sausage; consumers requiring halal-certified products should verify with the restaurant. 6. The price of SGD 24.90 from the field survey source likely reflects a set meal or bundle rather than a single sausage item; this field is preserved from the input but not used in nutritional calculations. 7. Multilingual names are provided per Singaporean cuisine rules (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi). Chinese names (金皮香肠 / 金皮香腸) are descriptive translations meaning 'golden skin sausage'. 8. All -1 sentinel values in per_100g represent nutrients not reported in the cited USDA SR Legacy source for this food category.
